target.name: Verkor

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $700M - $2.0B.

private.name: Watershed

private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series C), post-money $700M

data.narrative:
Verkor is a Series C-stage climate and energy technology company that has raised over €3B to date. The valuation analysis draws from a peer set of four private companies at the same stage within Climate & Energy: KoBold Metals ($2.0B), Watershed ($1.8B), Electric Hydrogen ($1.0B), and Amogy ($700M).

The peer-median method is the primary applicable triangulation approach given data constraints. The median valuation across these four same-sector, same-stage comparables is $1.4B, with a range spanning $700M to $2.0B. This peer cohort represents the most direct valuation benchmark available for Verkor's stage and sector positioning.

Based on peer-median triangulation, the analysis supports a valuation range of $1.2B (low) to $1.6B (high), with a central estimate of $1.4B. Key uncertainties include the precision of peer-stage classification, variation in technology maturity and capital efficiency across the Climate & Energy landscape, and limited availability of additional methodological anchors. The single-method foundation reflects data constraints; a more robust analysis would incorporate revenue multiple or DCF methodologies to cross-validate the peer-derived range.
